title: The Line of Best Fit
concept: Linear regression finds a straight line that minimizes the total distance to all data points on a scatterplot.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What makes regression different from classification?

options.text: Regression predicts continuous numbers, while classification predicts category labels.
Regression only works with images, while classification works with numbers.
Regression groups data without any target labels.

exercises.explain: Perfect recap! Regression predicts continuous numbers, like prices or times, while classification sorts data into buckets.

cards.title: Connecting the dots
cards.body: Imagine looking at a scatterplot showing house prices versus house size. The points are scattered everywhere. How do you summarize the relationship with just one simple path?

cards.title: The line of best fit
cards.body: In linear regression, the model draws a straight line through the data. This is called the line of best fit. It is positioned so that it gets as close as possible to all data points simultaneously.

cards.title: Stretching a rubber band
cards.body: Imagine every data point is a tiny peg, and you stretch a stiff rod through them, connected to each peg by a tiny rubber band. The rod will naturally settle where the pull from all the rubber bands balances out.

cards.title: Predicting coffee sales
cards.body: If you plot daily temperature against coffee sales, you'll see a downward trend. The line of best fit lets you input any temperature (even one you haven't seen before) and find the predicted sales on the line.

cards.title: It rarely hits the points
cards.body: You might think the line of best fit must touch most of the data points. In fact, it often touches none of them! Its job is to capture the overall trend, not to visit every individual house or temperature.

cards.title: Trend line vs. connecting dots
cards.body: Why we use a straight line instead of connecting every point:

left.text:
Captures general trend
Ignores minor noise
Simple & makes predictions

right.text:
Perfect on past data
Overfits to noise
Useless for general trends

exercises.prompt: What is the primary goal of the line of best fit?

options.text: To get as close as possible to all data points on average
To pass through every single data point in the dataset
To separate different categories of data with a boundary
To connect the lowest point to the highest point directly

exercises.explain: The line of best fit represents the general trend by minimizing the total distance to all data points.

exercises.prompt: True or false: The line of best fit must pass through at least half of the data points.
exercises.explain: False. The line captures the overall trend and might not touch a single actual data point.

exercises.sentence: Linear regression draws a ___ line through data points to capture their relationship.
exercises.explain: Linear regression specifically uses a straight line to map the relationship between variables.

exercises.prompt: Which tool does linear regression use to make a prediction for a new input?
exercises.explain: To predict, the model finds the input value on the horizontal axis and reads the corresponding height on the line.

exercises.prompt: You plot years of experience against salary for 100 software engineers. The points are scattered, but generally go up from left to right. When the linear regression model finishes training, it finds a line of best fit. How does this line make a prediction for a new engineer with 5 years of experience?

options.text: It looks up 5 years on the line and reads the corresponding salary value from the line.
It averages the salaries of the two closest engineers in the dataset.
It guesses a salary between the absolute minimum and maximum in the dataset.
It demands more features before it can output a line.

exercises.explain: Splendid! The line of best fit serves as a lookup formula: you input the feature (years of experience) and get the predicted target (salary) directly from the line's height. Let's see how the model actually adjusts this line using two special knobs next!
